prompt:
Weekend escalation from the flower-delivery marketplace. Support has three tickets they swear are unrelated; ops swears the deploy was clean because everything compiles and most orders flow fine. All three are biting real customers:

1) Promo desk: the '$75 and up ships free' promo is charging the $12.99 delivery fee to carts that land EXACTLY on $75.00. Ticket #4417 is three $25.00 peony dozens — customer got charged $87.99. Finance checked: $75.01 carts ship free, $74.99 carts correctly pay. It is only ever the on-the-nose carts.

2) Partner onboarding: we onboarded twelve delivery zones across four regions last month, but the coverage report insists we cover FOUR zones. Worse, orders for Meadowbrook (zone NE-11) keep printing at the Verbena North shop across town, and Petal & Stem Uptown (NE-04) hasn't seen an order all week. Every region with several zones funnels everything to one shop; regions where we only have one shop behave perfectly.

3) Dispatch promises are chaos since we expanded past the east coast. A Denver customer who ordered at 1:30 pm — half an hour BEFORE the 2 pm same-day cutoff — was promised next-day delivery. Honolulu is worse: no order ever books same-day there, no matter how early. Meanwhile a Manhattan order placed at 11:59 pm got labeled same-day. The cutoff is supposed to be 2 pm at the fulfilling shop, wherever that shop is.

The pipeline is the three implementation files in this directory: Order.java (cart pricing and the promo), RegionRouter.java (zone-to-shop routing), DispatchScheduler.java (the delivery promise). Find whatever is actually wrong and fix it. Keep the fixes surgical — no rewrites, no API changes; confirmation strings, error messages, and report ordering must stay exactly as they are. TestMain.java is the acceptance contract; don't touch it. Done = `java TestMain.java` prints all PASS and exits 0.
